title: Dossier (Base)
description: Base schema for verifiable dossiers — issuer-curated, ACDC-native collections of evidence with no issuee. Any schema that derives from this one (via an allOf $ref to this schema's SAID) is considered a dossier schema.

u.description: Optional salt/nonce supporting blinded or graduated disclosure.

i.description: AID of the issuer — the curator who attests to the composition of the evidence collection.

rd.description: SAID of the issuance/revocation registry, if one is in use.

s.description: SAID of the schema to which this dossier conforms.

a.description: Proximate metadata about the dossier itself. MUST NOT carry primary evidence (which belongs in `e`). SHOULD NOT include an `i` (issuee) field.

oneOf.description: Compact form: SAID of the attributes block.

assemble_dt.description: When the evidence collection was assembled (may precede issuance).

assembler.description: AID or human-readable name of the assembler, if distinct from the issuer.

purpose.description: Plain-language statement of why the dossier was assembled.

ref.description: External reference identifier (case number, docket, transaction ID, etc.).

gov.description: SAID or URI of the governance framework under which the dossier was assembled.

evt_dt.description: When the documented event or incident occurred.

evt_loc.description: Where the event or subject matter occurred. Format is domain-dependent.

jur.description: The legal or regulatory jurisdiction (not a geographic location) within which the dossier's subject matter falls, or under whose authority the evidence was collected. Expressed as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code identifying the sovereign jurisdiction, optionally extended with an ISO 3166-2 subdivision code identifying a sub-jurisdiction (e.g., `US-TX`, `FR`, `CA-ON`). MAY be an array where multiple jurisdictions apply.

class.description: Domain-dependent category or type identifier (offense code, NTSB event type, case type, etc.).

phase.description: Procedural maturity at time of issuance (e.g., preliminary, factual, final).

gov_rules.description: SAID or URI of the procedural ruleset that governed evidence collection.

e.description: Edges — references to external evidence and (optionally) prior versions, annotations, or joint-issuance operator groups. Primary evidence MUST enter the dossier through this section, not through `a`.

oneOf.description: Compact form: SAID of the edges block.
